title: BlackBerry FY26 Turnaround Complete, QNX Achieves 14% Growth
text: BlackBerry declared its transformation successful in its fiscal 2026 earnings presentation on April 9, 2026. The company reported fourth quarter revenue of $156.0 million, up 10% year-over-year, with full-year revenue reaching $549.1 million. BlackBerry achieved eight consecutive quarters of profitability improvement, generating $24.3 million in net income for Q4 and $53.2 million for the full fiscal year. Adjusted EBITDA margins expanded to 20% for fiscal 2026, totaling $107.1 million, a 27% year-over-year increase. Both operating divisions achieved Rule of 40 performance—a software industry benchmark combining growth rate and profit margin. The QNX embedded operating system division revenue reached $268.0 million, representing 14% year-over-year growth. The fourth quarter proved particularly strong at $78.7 million, up 20% year-over-year. The QNX royalty backlog expanded to approximately $950 million, exceeding twice the division's annual royalty recognition rate. Notable design wins during the quarter included partnerships with tier-one suppliers for the Chinese market and digital cockpit wins with top-five global automakers. QNX is positioned as foundational infrastructure for emerging Physical AI market. Beyond automotive, QNX secured recognition from Johnson & Johnson for an AI-enabled heart pump and adoption by major North American industrial automation providers. The Secure Communications division returned to growth with fourth quarter revenue of $72.5 million, up 8% year-over-year. Full-year revenue reached $258.9 million with annual recurring revenue of $218 million, up 5% year-over-year. BlackBerry ended fiscal 2026 with $432.4 million in total cash and investments. The company generated $50.3 million in operating cash flow for the full year and repurchased $60 million of shares since May 2025.
